strengths: Demonstrated hands-on experience building agentic workflows and AI infrastructure at re:cinq, including MCP code generators.
Strong backend and distributed systems background with real-time, high-throughput systems from Optilyz and Dunnhumby.
Proven track record in open-source technical leadership and tooling development (GISCollective, Fluent-Asserts, Trial).
concerns: Current career gap since October 2025 raises questions about recent active technical engagement.
No explicit evidence of multi-agent AI systems deployed in regulated fintech environments or with financial risk consequences.
Lacks clear demonstration of scaling engineering teams from 10 to 30+ or leading senior technical leadership layers.
response.justification: The candidate brings strong technical depth in distributed systems and emerging agentic tooling, with relevant open-source contributions and full-stack execution, but lacks direct fintech regulatory experience and documented leadership in scaling engineering teams — critical gaps for an executive CTO role in regulated AI-driven finance.
